# Supercharger

> Local-first technical support training and job prep where an AI coding assistant generates structured learning tracks, realistic support tickets, Docker sandbox lessons, and checkpoint validation.

Supercharger is an open-source technical readiness platform for support engineers, solutions engineers, SREs, TAMs, DevRel teams, junior engineers, and technical practitioners preparing for support-heavy interviews or onboarding.

Key pages:

- [What is Supercharger?](https://supercharger.richpolanco.com/introduction/what-is-supercharger/)
- [Quickstart](https://supercharger.richpolanco.com/introduction/quickstart/)
- [Prepare me for this job](https://supercharger.richpolanco.com/guides/job-prep/)
- [Working through a lesson](https://supercharger.richpolanco.com/guides/lessons/)
- [Generating a track](https://supercharger.richpolanco.com/guides/generating-a-track/)
- [Product Support Specialist case study](https://supercharger.richpolanco.com/case-studies/product-support-specialist/)
- [Content spec](https://supercharger.richpolanco.com/reference/content-spec/)

Repository:

- [GitHub: mrpolanco/Supercharger](https://github.com/mrpolanco/Supercharger)

Core concepts:

- Tracks are plain markdown/YAML folders generated by an AI coding assistant against `SPEC.md` and `AGENTS.md`.
- Preps turn job postings and optional resumes into requirement maps, gap-aware study plans, interview prep, curriculum order, and suggested new tracks.
- Hands-on lessons run in disposable Docker sandboxes and are validated by lesson-specific check scripts.
- Supercharger is local-first: no accounts, no telemetry, no API keys, and content remains plain files.
